channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Screenbound launches September 10
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Also available via Game Pass.

Dual-world action adventure Screenbound will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store on September 10, publisher and developer Crescent Moon Games and publisher Digital Pajamas announced. It will also be available via Game Pass.

Here is an overview of the game, via Crescent Moon Games:

About

You’ve discovered the Qboy, a strange device you found in your mom’s garage. This handheld console is unlike any other because when you power it on, reality splits!

Screenbound is a dual-world action adventure where a 2D handheld game and the 3D world around it exist at the same time — and you control both.

Embark on an adventure through this unknown world, switching between the realistic 3D world and a retro-styled 2D world, solving puzzles and defeating enemies while uncovering an exciting story in an unknown world.

Screenbound blends retro-inspired pixel art with a stylized 3D world and looks to bring a new and exciting take on the platforming genre, challenging you to take information from the 3D world you’re in, and the 2D world on your screen, to overcome the 5D challenges put in front of you.

This isn’t just a game. It’s a quantum experiment and you’re the variable.

Key Features

  • Dual-Dimensional Gameplay – Control both 3D and 2D versions of yourself simultaneously. Your actions mirror across realities, but the rules don’t.
  • Challenging Puzzles – Solve different puzzles that require shifting perspective, timing and logic across two planes of existence.
  • Unlock New Tools and Skills – Unlock new abilities as you progress that give you even more ways to solve the puzzles
  • Uncover the Story and Secrets – Explore offbeat areas and discover a unique story through environmental storytelling.
  • Dual Focus – Some objects, enemies, and platforms only exist in one world so stay alert!


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/screenbound-launches-september-10
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ‘Slap-and-solve adventure’ game Slap Out Of It! announced for consoles, PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

From Floor Plan developer Turbo Button.

Floor Plan developer Turbo Button has announced Slap Out Of It!, a “slap-and-solve adventure” game set in a “slapstick comedy sandbox packed with silly puzzles.” It will launch for unspecified consoles and PC via Steam in 2027.

“Our team is dedicated to interactive comedy and celebrating the art of dumb,” said Turbo Button co-founder Holden Link in a press release. “Up until now, we’ve been known for making a bunch of silly [virtual reality] games, but Slap Out of It! is our first time stepping away from [virtual reality] entirely. It builds upon our past work, while exploring lots of new ideas, and it’s all meticulously hand crafted to keep you thinking and laughing at the same time.”

  • ❮
  • ❯

Here is an overview of the game, via Turbo Button:

About

Slap Out Of It! is a slap-and-solve adventure that stretches your wits and your noodly arms in all directions. Explore impossible towers where every floor is a comedy sandbox of secrets. Meet strange characters and solve (or add to) their problems with a personal touch. The power to change everything is in your hands.

Story

Our world is broken… but at Puzzl, we’re putting it back together piece by piece – better than before! Join our team of PuzzPals and help us solve the world’s problems with a personal touch. But first, our CEO needs some help with a very important mission. You’re not afraid of heights, right?

An Interactive Comedy Sandbox

You see that mountain? You can slap it. Well, you could if this game had mountains. Everything within reach of your stretchy arms is satisfyingly interactive and responsive to your touch with animation, sound, and haptics. Every background detail is intentional and worthy of your attention—so explore and slap everything, because you never know what might happen. Maybe we’ll add mountains.

Lateral and Vertical Thinking

Lean on the skills you’ve learned from point-and-clicks to SLAP-AND-SOLVE. Use your elevator to travel between floors, gather items in your fanny pack, and make logical leaps as you unravel how every floor and their inhabitants are connected. And if you get stuck, keep on slapping or ask the operator for some intel to help you along.

A “Floor Plan” adventure

Slap Out Of It! is inspired and adapted from “Floor Plan,” the hit VR escape game series. It has been dramatically expanded with all-new gameplay and secrets to discover, and meticulously redesigned from the ground up for keyboard and controller. Please note that this is not a virtual reality game and does not have any virtual reality support.

Key Features

  • A comedy sandbox with puzzles and surprises hidden behind every slap.
  • Think with your hands to solve and progress through interconnected escape rooms.
  • Embrace cartoon logic as you explore, touch, and smack everything in sight.
  • Meet a huge cast of bizarre characters, and solve (or become) their problems.
  • Six to 10 handcrafted hours of gloriously stupid jokes and memorable “aha” moments.
  • A Floor Plan adventure—inspired by the hit escape game series, now with 100 percent less virtual reality.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/slap-and-solve-adventure-game-slap-out-of-it-announced-for-consoles-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Interactive mystery thriller Apple Crumble announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

From Duck Detective developer Happy Broccoli Games.

Duck Detective series developer Happy Broccoli Games has announced Apple Crumble, a fully-voiced interactive mystery thriller inspired by titles like MOUTHWASHING and Knives Out. It will launch for PC via Steam in 2026.

Here is an overview of the game, via Happy Broccoli Games:

About

SOMEONE’S TAKING A SLICE!

Apple Crumble is a suspenseful interactive mystery set during your grandma’s 84th birthday party. One of the guests is out to knife your Nan, but the question is—who?

It’s down to you to uncover the truth. Explore your childhood home, reconnect with your dysfunctional family, and work out who you can trust before it’s time for tea in this short, twisty thriller from the creators of Duck Detective.

Featuring a fully voiced cast of suspects:

  • Your beloved grandma
  • Your stressed-out mum
  • Your midlife crisis uncle
  • The weird man in your bedroom
  • You

Key Features

  • 60 to 90 minute long mystery thriller walking simulator.
  • Fully voice-acted.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Teaser Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/interactive-mystery-thriller-apple-crumble-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO announced for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC
item.content:encoded:
Metanet Software has announced N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO, its latest physics-based high-speed platforming game in the N series and the “ultimate virtual couch party game.” It will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC via Steam in 2027.

Here is an overview of the game, via Metanet Software:

N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO has remixed the N series from the ground up with its unmistakable formula now perfected for virtual athletics.

Master momentum as you dash across beautifully handcrafted minimalist levelscapes, avoiding obstacles and dodging deviously designed enemies. N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO hands would-be warriors infinite replayability and the highest level of competition yet in the N series, in this multi-layered multiplayer parkour platformer.

Originating as a flash game, N brought peerless platforming to over 100 million web surfers in the mid-aughts, with future entries in the franchise further refining the game’s tight, hardcore mechanics while folding in more cooperative play and competitive game modes. The new N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO is the cumulation of more than twenty years of experience, and Metanet’s take on a party platforming game where the problem posed by every level is a playground waiting for players.

Even things that aren’t your enemies can kill you—take fall damage, get pancaked on the ceiling, sabotage other players and manipulate the enemies in a deadly ballet of movement as you dash to victory. A menacing menagerie of meanies react to your every move in this smooth and unique platformer from the indie platforming trailblazers at Metanet.

  • ❮
  • ❯

The return of the “World’s Best Platformer” (TM) brings players multiple game modes perfect for testing their reflexes with multi-layered parkour platforming action: Five fresh flavors of online (and local) running (and jumping) await.

  • Trials and Cooperative Play – N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO will still have the staples of the N franchise with the return of Trials, a reworking of the solo and hardcore modes of previous N games, and cooperative play, along with a remixed version of Racing (lovingly referred to as Rocket Murder Time) as well as two other brand-new competitive game modes that will test friendships in some of the platforming’s most pulse-pounding action: One-Up and Team Tag.
  • Races – Races in N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO keep up the blazing fast action fans will remember from past games, pushing up to four players to reach the exit of a level as quickly as possible, collecting gold along the way to boost their score, with one distinctive change. It’s not unlike a faster, sleeker version of Fall Guys, except when you reach the finish line you can blast a bazooka to blow your opponents back to the starting line.
  • One-Up – N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO‘s first new game mode, pushes players to constantly perform better in fast-paced push-your-luck elimination contests. Get the most gold and reach the exit faster than someone else, and you’ll stay in the game. One trip up spells the end though, leaving you waiting for the next round.
  • Team Tag – Introduces the crown jewel of N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO as teams of two think cooperatively and coordinate strategically to hunt each other down. Ninjas must evade Hunters while collecting gold to raise their score, with each team switching roles between rounds to see who can earn the most and survive the longest. A deceptively simple concept, Team Tag quickly becomes a fast-paced game of chess as each team tries to outmaneuver the other.

Elevating the N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO experience are the franchise’s staple visual and auditory delights. Visuals consist of Metanet’s trademark minimalist style that can be swapped between dozens of different color palettes with their own tracing and particle effects that make each level and ninja pop off the screen. Players can also customize their own ninja with Funlockable™ cosmetics that are earned by simply playing.

In signature Metanet style, the game’s soundtrack comes with a megakiller globe-spanning soundtrack of 50+ hand-selected artists like Dusky and Cameo Blush, providing the perfect audio soundscape for precision running, jumping and failing.

Sourced by hand by Metanet from the smallest alleyways of Bandcamp, the soundtrack’s curation spans musicians around the globe, fueling an eclectic and offensively tasteful mix of electronic audio delights across a multitude of genres, subgenres and sub-sub genres.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/n-plus-infinity-times-two-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-2-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Messhof announces 2D platforming survival game Blood Dungeon for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Due out in the late summer.

Nidhogg developer Messhof has announced Blood Dungeon, a 2D platforming survival game due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam in the late summer. A demo is available now for PC.

“I like to sketch ideas in Game Maker once in a while to get them out of my head,” says Messhof co-founder Mark Essen in a press release. “While it’s been a while since I’ve done the art for a Messhof project, I still enjoy working in 2D for fun side projects. This game started as a little weekend project that I shared with friends and lent itself to being something I could scratch at over the years as a way to wind down at night. The lack of pressure and small windows of time that defined 90% of the development kept it fun and drove a lot of the design decisions. I had a little $50 tablet with a screen and I did a lot of animation in the car if I had to wait around for something or other.”

  • ❮
  • ❯

Here is an overview of the game, via Messhoft:

About

From the creators of Nidhogg comes a game about 2D platforming survival. You’ll have to parkour your way around treacherous dungeons to kite monsters while gathering blood and powerups.

Survive a run, take the bones you’ve earned to the Bone Shop, and get stronger.

Complete quests to unlock upgrades, arenas, skins, and more.

Key Features

  • Nine unlockable playable characters with different traits and weapons.
  • Six unlockable unique survival arenas.
  • Over 100 enemy types.
  • Over 100 quests to complete.
  • Over 100 unlockable weapons and upgrades.
  • Dynamic “dungeon dnb” soundtrack by Thomas Hooey.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/messhof-announces-2d-platforming-survival-game-blood-dungeon-for-ps5-xbox-series-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
